title: chrysocephalum apiculatum subsp. pilbarense
intro: Chrysocephalum apiculatum subsp. pilbarense is a subspecies of the Australian bush daisy plant, characterized by its yellow flowers and narrow leaves. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as relieving colds and flu, soothing sore throats, and reducing fever. Chrysocephalum apiculatum subsp. pilbarense can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
